rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only identifiers and types present in the AADL model; avoid introducing package-qualified names or undefined references.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope rules when inserting AGREE contracts: component ports and data types must be valid in the target scope.
Do not encode AADL properties (e.g., Period, Priority) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Avoid unsafe type conversions or numeric encodings of Boolean or event ports; only use model-provided values.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports in valid scopes; bindings are for implementation-level subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL property references that are not present in the analyzed model.
Do not encode event ports or Boolean data as numeric values unless explicitly defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
Defensive rule diagnostics for Boolean and event port handling
